findings.Description: The fallback function allows reentrancy attacks by performing an external ETH transfer before completing state updates, appearing in both the smart contract code and static analysis results. This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Attackers could re-enter the function to drain ETH and multiply tokens, potentially stealing funds and disrupting token economics.

findings.Description: The destroycontract function allows any caller to destroy the contract and send remaining ETH, appearing in both the smart contract code and static analysis results. This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Anyone can permanently destroy the contract and steal remaining ETH, leading to complete loss of funds and contract functionality.

findings.Description: The contract inherits SafeM but doesn't consistently use it for arithmetic operations, appearing in both the smart contract code and static analysis results. This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Potential arithmetic overflows/underflows in transfer and burn functions could lead to incorrect token accounting.

findings.Description: The approveAndCall function makes external calls to untrusted addresses before state finalization, appearing in both the smart contract code and static analysis results. This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Malicious contracts could re-enter and manipulate approvals, potentially draining approved funds.

findings.Description: Transfer functions incorrectly multiply values by 10^8 twice (in transfer() and constructor), appearing in the smart contract code only. This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Token accounting errors and inconsistent behavior between different transfer functions.

findings.Description: The fallback function performs msg.value * 50000 without overflow protection or decimal scaling, appearing in both the smart contract code and static analysis results. This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Potential integer overflow and incorrect token minting calculations.

findings.Description: The Airdrop function lacks array length checks and amount validation, appearing in both the smart contract code and static analysis results. This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Could lead to incorrect airdrops or out-of-gas errors with large arrays.

findings.Description: Burn functions lack minimum value checks and have inconsistent decimal handling, appearing in both the smart contract code and static analysis results. This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Potential incorrect event logging and near-zero value burns affecting token accounting.

findings.Description: No minimum/maximum transfer value checks implemented, appearing in both the smart contract code and static analysis results. This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Potential dust attacks and accidental large transfers affecting token economics.
